materials_block.text: Xanthone (XAN), PLGA (50:50) MW 50,000–75,000, Pluronic F-68, phosphate buffered saline tablets and soybean lecithin (40% purity) were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich. Threalose dihydrate from Fluka. 3-MeOXAN synthesized in-house. Myritol 318 supplied by Henkel. Methanol/acetonitrile from Merck. Water purified by Milli-Q.

preparation_blocks.text: PLGA (63 mg) and XAN or 3-MeOXAN dissolved in acetone (10 mL) → added to aqueous Pluronic F-68 0.25% → stirred → solvent removed → centrifugation → redispersion. Empty nanospheres prepared without drug.
PLGA (~50 mg) + lecithin (100 mg) in acetone → drug dissolved in Myritol 318 → mixed → poured into Pluronic F-68 0.5% → solvent removal → ultrafiltration. Empty nanocapsules without drug.

formulation_tables.description: Encapsulation parameters of XAN and 3-MeOXAN in PLGA nanospheres
Encapsulation parameters of XAN and 3-MeOXAN in PLGA nanocapsules
Formulations of nanocapsules including composition and properties

property_tables.description: Mean diameter, PDI and zeta potential of nanospheres
Mean diameter, PDI and zeta potential of nanocapsules

optimization_blocks.text: Loading capacity evaluated with varying drug amounts. Maximum efficiency at 60 μg/mL. Above 70 μg/mL precipitation occurs. Indicates saturation and low affinity to polymer.
Drug concentration varied (200–800 μg/mL for XAN, 1000–1600 μg/mL for 3-MeOXAN). Efficiency ~90% and ~80%. Saturation observed at higher concentrations. Solubility in oil core governs loading.

experimental_design_signal.text: fixed polymer/surfactant + varying XAN or 3-MeOXAN concentration across batches
